meanings.definition: be relieved of one's duties temporarily

examples: The spy sent the classified information off to Russia

meanings.definition: cause to be acquitted; get off the hook; in a legal case
examples: The lawyer got him off, even though there was no doubt in everybody's mind that he killed his wife

meanings.definition: escape potentially unpleasant consequences; get away with a forbidden action
examples: I couldn't get out from under these responsibilities
The officer hopped out when he spotted an illegally parked car
I'll mail you the check tomorrow
He got off the best line I've heard in a long time
